title: Léon Gaucherel

infobox.value: (21 May 1816, [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris") – 7 January 1886, Paris) was a French painter and etcher.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

French painter (1816–1886)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bf/Courtry-Gaucherel.jpg/250px-Courtry-Gaucherel.jpg)](/wiki/File:Courtry-Gaucherel.jpg)

Léon Gaucherel, by  
[Charles Courtry](/w/index.php?title=Charles_Courtry&action=edit&redlink=1 "Charles Courtry (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Charles_Courtry "fr:Charles Courtry")] (1871)

**Léon Gaucherel** (21 May 1816, [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris") – 7 January 1886, Paris) was a French painter and etcher.

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=L%C3%A9on_Gaucherel&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

He learned art from his childhood friend, [Eugène Viollet-le-Duc](/wiki/Eug%C3%A8ne_Viollet-le-Duc "Eugène Viollet-le-Duc"), whom he accompanied to Sicily from 1836 to 1837.[[1]](#cite_note-1) His first major works were illustrations for a project to restore [Notre-Dame](/wiki/Notre-Dame_de_Paris "Notre-Dame de Paris"), proposed by his friend and [Jean-Baptiste Lassus](/wiki/Jean-Baptiste_Lassus "Jean-Baptiste Lassus") in 1843. The following year, he began to master the art of [etching](/wiki/Etching "Etching").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Over time, he went from producing original engravings to making reproductions of works by other artists. By 1858, the transition was complete and he began creating illustrations for magazines; notably the *[Gazette des Beaux-Arts](/wiki/Gazette_des_Beaux-Arts "Gazette des Beaux-Arts")* and the *Annales Archéologiques*, where he depicted decorative architectural details. His work on the Gazette, together with his fellow printmaker [Léopold Flameng](/wiki/L%C3%A9opold_Flameng "Léopold Flameng"), helped to establish that publication's reputation.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

He was an active member of the [Société des aquafortistes](/w/index.php?title=Soci%C3%A9t%C3%A9_des_aquafortistes&action=edit&redlink=1 "Société des aquafortistes (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Soci%C3%A9t%C3%A9_des_aquafortistes "fr:Société des aquafortistes")] and, in 1864, was named a Knight in the [Legion of Honor](/wiki/Legion_of_Honor "Legion of Honor").[[4]](#cite_note-4) In 1866 he created a large album of [still-lifes](/wiki/Still-life "Still-life") to promote etching, which was still considered to be more of a craft than an art. The latest techniques were introduced in an exhibition at the [Salon](/wiki/Salon_(Paris) "Salon (Paris)").

In 1875, he became the artistic director for the weekly revue, *[L'Art](/w/index.php?title=L%27Art_(revue_illustr%C3%A9e)&action=edit&redlink=1 "L'Art (revue illustrée) (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/L%27Art_(revue_illustr%C3%A9e) "fr:L'Art (revue illustrée)")]*, founded by [Eugène Véron](/w/index.php?title=Eug%C3%A8ne_V%C3%A9ron&action=edit&redlink=1 "Eugène Véron (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eug%C3%A8ne_V%C3%A9ron "fr:Eugène Véron")]. During his tenure, he recruited several engravers to be permanent staff members. Among the most popular was [Auguste-Hilaire Léveillé](/w/index.php?title=Auguste-Hilaire_L%C3%A9veill%C3%A9&action=edit&redlink=1 "Auguste-Hilaire Léveillé (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Auguste-Hilaire_L%C3%A9veill%C3%A9 "fr:Auguste-Hilaire Léveillé")]. In addition to his creative activities, he also took numerous students, including [Victor Gustave Lhuillier](/wiki/Victor_Gustave_Lhuillier "Victor Gustave Lhuillier"), [Louis Monzies](/wiki/Louis_Monzies "Louis Monzies"), [Edmond Ramus](/wiki/Edmond_Ramus "Edmond Ramus"), and [Adolphe Lalauze](/wiki/Adolphe_Lalauze "Adolphe Lalauze").

The museum of art and history in [Sainte-Menehould](/wiki/Sainte-Menehould "Sainte-Menehould") has preserved many of his original plates.

## Selected works

* [![Basilique Notre-Dame-de-Paradis [fr]](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2f/Hennebont_%C3%A9glise_par_l%C3%A9on_gaucherel_1.jpg/250px-Hennebont_%C3%A9glise_par_l%C3%A9on_gaucherel_1.jpg)](/wiki/File:Hennebont_%C3%A9glise_par_l%C3%A9on_gaucherel_1.jpg "Basilique Notre-Dame-de-Paradis [fr]")

  [Basilique Notre-Dame-de-Paradis](/w/index.php?title=Basilique_Notre-Dame-de-Paradis&action=edit&redlink=1 "Basilique Notre-Dame-de-Paradis (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Basilique_Notre-Dame-de-Paradis "fr:Basilique Notre-Dame-de-Paradis")]

  The roof of the [Hospices de Beaune](/wiki/Hospices_de_Beaune "Hospices de Beaune")
